b' Hegseth on the Brink, DeSantis Swap Theory & Kennedy Kinks
Welcome back to The Best & The Brightest. Mar-a-Lago has stepped up leak patrol ever since Todd Blanche, the nominee for deputy A.G., was spotted at Swifty\xe2\x80\x99s in Palm Beach on Tuesday having a poolside lunch with CNN\xe2\x80\x99s Kaitlan Collins, who has been sourcing up around town. Raheem Kassam, the editor of The National Pulse and the guy who tweeted photos of the two, has been pressured by Trump aides to delete the post out of fear that it could tank Blanche\xe2\x80\x99s standing with the president-elect and thus, his chance of even making it to the confirmation process. After all, Trump gets furious about leakers, perhaps because he prefers to pick up the phone and do the leaking himself. \xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c

Welcome back to The Best & The Brightest. I\xe2\x80\x99m Tara Palmeri .

Mar-a-Lago has stepped up leak patrol ever since Todd Blanche , the nominee for deputy A.G., was spotted at Swifty\xe2\x80\x99s in Palm Beach on Tuesday having a poolside lunch with CNN\xe2\x80\x99s Kaitlan Collins , who has been sourcing up around town. Raheem Kassam , the editor of The National Pulse and the guy who tweeted photos of the two, has been pressured by Trump aides to delete the post out of fear that it could tank Blanche\xe2\x80\x99s standing with the president-elect and thus, his chance of even making it to the confirmation process. After all, Trump gets furious about leakers, perhaps because he prefers to pick up the phone and do the leaking himself.

In tonight\xe2\x80\x99s issue, news and notes on the faltering nomination of Pete Hegseth and the potential domino effect it might have on other problematic Trump nominees.

But first\xe2\x80\xa6

\xf0\x9f\x8e\xa7 Somebody\xe2\x80\x99s Gotta Win : A quick plug for my podcast yesterday with Joe Hack , the former Republican chief of staff turned lobbyist, about the kinks in the confirmation process for Trump\xe2\x80\x99s most controversial nominees. Joe knows more than almost anyone about which senators to watch as the deliberations unfold. Listen here or here .

A MESSAGE FROM INSTAGRAM
Instagram Teen Accounts: a protected experience for teens, guided by parents

Instagram Teen Accounts are designed to address parents\xe2\x80\x99 biggest concerns, providing automatic protections for who can contact their teens and the content they can see.

The impact: Built-in limits give parents more peace of mind when it comes to protecting their teens.

Learn More
\xf0\x9f\x92\x89 Speaking of : A few friends reached out to direct my attention to the\xe2\x80\xa6 unusual application form to work for R.F.K. Jr. , and then presumably at the Department of Health and Human Services, the agency he\xe2\x80\x99s been nominated to lead. The Make America Healthy Again intake form is certifiably comedic\xe2\x80\x94and a little gross.

The form asks candidates to answer questions by selecting responses such as:

  • \xe2\x80\x9cI don\xe2\x80\x99t have much interest in having sexual experiences with another person\xe2\x80\x9d
  • \xe2\x80\x9cI believe in things many others don\xe2\x80\x99t\xe2\x80\x94like having a \xe2\x80\x98sixth sense,\xe2\x80\x99 clairvoyance, and telepathy\xe2\x80\x94and as an adolescent, I had bizarre fantasies or preoccupations\xe2\x80\x9d and
  • \xe2\x80\x9cI consistently use my physical appearance to draw attention to myself.\xe2\x80\x9d
You can check the application out yourself here . When I reached out to the Trump transition team for comment, spokesman Brian Hughes confirmed the page is real and being used by Kennedy\xe2\x80\x99s team. \xe2\x80\x9cMany nominees and appointees are soliciting r\xc3\xa9sum\xc3\xa9s and then submitting them to the transition for the consideration and process,\xe2\x80\x9d he told me.

And now, on to the main event \xe2\x80\xa6
The Hegseth Sitch & Trump Confirmation Dominoes
The president-elect has stopped working the phones for Pete Hegseth, and Ron DeSantis may be on deck, amid cascading scrutiny of Trump\xe2\x80\x99s most controversial nominees.
TARA PALMERI
Last month, when Donald Trump first began his rapid-fire nomination of baggage-laden, barely vetted political allies to fill out his cabinet, Washington presumed a certain method to his madness. Sure, the likes of Matt Gaetz , Pete Hegseth , Tulsi Gabbard , and Robert F. Kennedy Jr. seemed obviously unqualified. But Trump\xe2\x80\x99s straight-from-the-gut, spaghetti-at-the-wall decision-making at least suggested a patina of strategy. Republican senators, stunned into submission by the onslaught, appeared ready to capitulate in a humbling demonstration of loyalty. \xe2\x80\x9cIf Donald Trump says jump three feet high and scratch your head, we all jump three feet high and scratch our heads,\xe2\x80\x9d Rep. Troy Nehls declared, one week after the election. \xe2\x80\x9cAnd that\xe2\x80\x99s it.\xe2\x80\x9d

Now, of course, a new conventional wisdom is emerging. Gaetz, under investigation for drug use and sexual misconduct with a minor, withdrew himself from contention for attorney general. Chad Chronister , nominated to run the D.E.A., also took himself out of consideration. And Republican senators\xe2\x80\x94it will only take four to tank a nominee come January\xe2\x80\x94have become somewhat more emboldened with each new fallen domino.

The next Trump nominee to go down may be Hegseth, who\xe2\x80\x99s been tapped to run the Pentagon. After failing to win over Joni Ernst during his Capitol Hill charm campaign yesterday, I\xe2\x80\x99m told by Senate sources that he may be forced to withdraw from consideration by the end of the week. Ernst, a combat veteran and sexual assault survivor, had told reporters she would need to have a serious conversation with Hegseth, who has been accused of sexual assault and stated publicly that women should not serve in combat. (Hegseth has denied the accusations of assault.) Earlier today on Fox News, Ernst said she wasn\xe2\x80\x99t ready to ratify him. \xe2\x80\x9cThe vetting will continue,\xe2\x80\x9d she added.
A MESSAGE FROM INSTAGRAM
Instagram Teen Accounts: automatic protections for teens

Parents want safer online experiences for their teens. That's why Instagram is introducing Teen Accounts, with automatic protections for who can contact teens and the content they can see.

A key factor: Only parents can approve safety setting changes for teens under 16.

Learn More
Meanwhile, Hegseth said on Wednesday that Trump had called him beforehand to express his unequivocal support. But it appears the president-elect is at least entertaining the idea of moving on. According to a quintuple-bylined story on ABC News, Trump\xe2\x80\x99s team has been discussing the possibility that Hegseth\xe2\x80\x99s nomination is no longer viable, and Trump himself isn\xe2\x80\x99t working the phones for the former Fox & Friends co-host like he did for Gaetz. \xe2\x80\x9cTrump\xe2\x80\x99s never had a huge degree of loyalty,\xe2\x80\x9d a senior Senate source told me. \xe2\x80\x9cIf it becomes a problem for him, like the Gaetz thing, they\xe2\x80\x99re done.\xe2\x80\x9d

Notably, I\xe2\x80\x99m told that Trump isn\xe2\x80\x99t much bothered that Hegseth has been accused of sexual assault (an experience they share). Instead, according to a source in Trumpworld, it\xe2\x80\x99s the tales of Hegseth\xe2\x80\x99s excessive drinking that may have become a dealbreaker. For all his sins, Trump is famously a teetotaler\xe2\x80\x94as are many of his closest aides\xe2\x80\x94and he blames alcoholism for the death of his brother, Fred , at only 42. \xe2\x80\x9cHis naysayers are focusing on the women, but that doesn\xe2\x80\x99t matter to Trump,\xe2\x80\x9d this source said. The issue is the booze.

Indeed, reports that Trump was flirting with tapping Ron DeSantis as Hegseth\xe2\x80\x99s replacement only surfaced after current and former coworkers at Fox News, and the veterans nonprofits he led, told NBC News they\xe2\x80\x99d smelled alcohol on Hegseth at work, or had seen him drunk at work functions\xe2\x80\x94allegations that a spokesperson for the transition called \xe2\x80\x9ccompletely unfounded and false.\xe2\x80\x9d

There\xe2\x80\x99s no doubt that DeSantis would be confirmable. The secretary of defense manages a global workforce of nearly 3 million people, and operates with a budget close to $850 million. DeSantis has spent the last several years as the governor of a state with some 22.6 million people, and is a military veteran himself. He\xe2\x80\x99s also been thoroughly vetted by the public and by the press. Meanwhile, Hegseth\xe2\x80\x99s managerial experience is limited to running two nonprofits, reportedly quite poorly.

Would DeSantis want the job if it were offered to him? There\xe2\x80\x99s always the specter of Mitt Romney , who lobbied for the secretary of state job at a very public dinner with Trump at Jean-Georges in New York, only to have Trump snatch away the proverbial football. (I\xe2\x80\x99m told Senator Bill Hagerty , who was previously considered for Defense, is still waiting in the wings.) DeSantis is on better terms with Trump\xe2\x80\x94he already completed his genuflections last year\xe2\x80\x94but it\xe2\x80\x99s not as if he has tons of allies inside Mar-a-Lago. Susie Wiles , the Florida super-operative and Trump\xe2\x80\x99s incoming chief of staff, isn\xe2\x80\x99t a fan of her former boss. And while Trump\xe2\x80\x99s \xe2\x80\x9cfirst buddy\xe2\x80\x9d Elon Musk donated $10 million to DeSantis\xe2\x80\x99s primary campaign, he hasn\xe2\x80\x99t won many personnel fights of late.

There\xe2\x80\x99s family politics to consider, too. As governor, it would fall to DeSantis to appoint a replacement for Senator Marco Rubio , assuming he is confirmed as secretary of state, which seems likely. I\xe2\x80\x99m told that Trump sees DeSantis as someone he can pressure to get his daughter-in-law, Lara Trump , in that seat, extending the family dynasty and installing another loyalist in the upper chamber. Lieutenant Governor Jeanette Nun\xcc\x83ez , who would replace DeSantis if he were to join the Trump administration, might not be quite as malleable.
Confirmation Dominoes
Alas, deep-sixing the Hegseth problem might only create other headaches for the Trump team. \xe2\x80\x9cOnce Hegseth gets out, there will be pressure on the next one,\xe2\x80\x9d said the senior Senate source. \xe2\x80\x9cIt\xe2\x80\x99s going to be one story after the next.\xe2\x80\x9d

Tulsi Gabbard , Trump\xe2\x80\x99s pick for director of national intelligence, could be the next domino to fall, even before getting a committee hearing. Gabbard\xe2\x80\x99s history of pro- Putin and pro- Assad statements\xe2\x80\x94including, among other things, blaming NATO for Russia\xe2\x80\x99s invasion of Ukraine and a goodwill trip to Syria in 2017, after Assad gassed his own people\xe2\x80\x94have alarmed members of both parties.
Then there\xe2\x80\x99s Trump\xe2\x80\x99s would-be F.B.I. director, Kash Patel , who comes armed with an enemies list but little in the way of credentials. There were encouraging signs for Kash today when Sen. John Cornyn said on the Hugh Hewitt Show that \xe2\x80\x9che will\xe2\x80\x9d be confirmed to lead the bureau. On the other hand, it\xe2\x80\x99s still early, and everyone\xe2\x80\x99s been a bit too distracted with other noms to fully focus on Patel. Within the bureau, the reaction to Patel\xe2\x80\x99s selection is decidedly mixed, according to a recently retired senior official at the F.B.I. Most F.B.I. officers and agents are politically conservative, like many in law enforcement, this person noted, and many would welcome a shake-up\xe2\x80\x94especially one that empowers field offices at the expense of D.C., where then-director Robert Mueller centralized many functions in the wake of 9/11.

But relocating key F.B.I. functions outside the capital is actually among the least controversial of Patel\xe2\x80\x99s proposals for the agency. He\xe2\x80\x99s also vowed to prosecute political opponents and members of the media, and to fashion himself as a sort of blunt weapon of Trump\xe2\x80\x99s campaign of retribution. I\xe2\x80\x99m already hearing justifications from some Trump allies, who note that the Obama administration did indeed put record numbers of leakers in prison and investigated but never prosecuted multiple journalists. The recently retired F.B.I. official speculated that Patel and Trump\xe2\x80\x99s new choice for attorney general, Pam Bondi , would investigate select leaks, and potentially subpoena reporters.

Even so, there\xe2\x80\x99s plenty there to make Republican senators second-guess the wisdom of putting Patel in charge of federal law enforcement. (Remember, it only takes four senators to kill a nomination\xe2\x80\x94and Susan Collins , Lisa Murkowski , and Mitch McConnell are just a few of several wild cards , as my partner Abby Livingston has reported.) That may take the pressure off the later round of Trump allies who will have to face the Senate, such as Heather Nauert , who served as Trump\xe2\x80\x99s acting undersecretary of state for public diplomacy and public affairs and is now being considered for a foreign policy position. (Nauert withdrew from consideration to be Trump\xe2\x80\x99s ambassador to the U.N., in 2019, when it was revealed that she\xe2\x80\x99d employed a nanny without the proper visa a decade prior\xe2\x80\x94a scandal that seems positively quaint by 2024 standards.)

Others are hanging around the hoop, waiting for a second or third round of cabinet flameouts to make their move. To wit: I\xe2\x80\x99m told that Ric Grenell , Trump\xe2\x80\x99s former acting director of national intelligence and ambassador to Germany, who has lobbied for a decade to be secretary of state, turned down an offer to serve as special envoy for resolving the Russia-Ukraine conflict. An ally familiar with his thinking said he didn\xe2\x80\x99t want to look like a \xe2\x80\x9csquish\xe2\x80\x9d to the MAGA base. Instead, I\xe2\x80\x99m told, he plans to remain in Trump\xe2\x80\x99s orbit and wait for Rubio to crash back to earth.
FOUR STORIES WE\xe2\x80\x99RE TALKING ABOUT
WaPo Editor Hunting
Plus, the story of Rashida Jones\xe2\x80\x99s impending MSNBC exit.
DYLAN BYERS
Musk Fever Dreams
Can a reverse merger save Trump\xe2\x80\x99s mediaco?
WILLIAM D. COHAN
Beauty\xe2\x80\x99s M&A Drought
On a year to forget for beauty industry dealmakers.
RACHEL STRUGATZ
The F.B.I. Kluster
A conversation about Kash Patel and the state of the bureau.
JOHN HEILEMANN
